0.3_to_0.4: Added unattributed_agency to secondary_tags allowed values
Added independent_genesis requirement to anchor_registry (RULE-005 update)
Updated document_metadata to replace preregistration hash mechanism with public repository fields
Added kill_switch field to each falsifiability_record entry
Added unattributed_agency to secondary_tags per session discussion

document_metadata._description: Top-level metadata for the document under analysis and the preregistration record covering this analysis.

date_of_document.notes: Date the source document was produced, not analysis date

analyst_id.notes: Anonymized; used for disagreement protocol only

preregistration_repo_url.notes: URL of the public GitHub repository holding the preregistration record. Replaces the prior hash mechanism. Public commit timestamp is the tamper-evident proof that priors were filed before analysis began.

preregistration_commit_hash.notes: The specific git commit hash of the first commit to the preregistration repo, before any document was touched analytically. This is the immutable chain-of-custody anchor.

preregistration_status.notes: Set to confirmed once the preregistration commit is live and the analyst has not yet begun analysis of this document. Schema violation to set confirmed after analysis has begun.

anchor_registry._description: One record per anchor used in this analysis. Anchors must be registered here before any proposition may reference them. No proposition may be analyzed without at least one anchor mapping.

anchor_text.notes: Verbatim statement of the anchor claim

classification_source.notes: Citation or description of what establishes this as an anchor

chain_length_from_governance_zone.notes: Number of inferential steps from the mathematical governance zone (r=0). Mathematical truths = 0. All other classifications require an integer — estimation is required, null is not permitted. If chain length cannot be estimated even approximately, the claim does not meet the bar for the anchor registry.

independent_genesis.notes: Required for all anchors. Documents whether at least one source establishing this anchor originates outside the administrative chain of command of the document under analysis.

independent_source_citation.notes: Required if independent_source_exists is true. Citation of the independent source.

genesis_confidence_weight.notes: full = at least one fully independent source exists. reduced = only sources within the same administrative chain exist; anchor is flagged but not disqualified; analyst must document the absence in the anchor record. minimal = provenance is unclear; anchor requires elevated scrutiny and a confidence_flag record.

genesis_note.notes: Required when independent_source_exists is false. Must state the nature of the institutional dependency and why independent corroboration is unavailable.

notes.notes: Optional. Caveats about scope or limits of this anchor.

anchor_classification_definitions.mathematical_truth: Formally provable, exogenous to all human manifolds. chain_length = 0, chain_length_confidence = exact.
anchor_classification_definitions.direct_empirical_record: Primary source documentation of a physical event (signed treaty, congressional record, census count).
anchor_classification_definitions.primary_source_attestation: First-person statement in a primary document verifiable against the document itself without interpretation.
anchor_classification_definitions.scientific_consensus: Proposition with no credible dissenting peer review at time of document production. chain_length must be estimated, chain_length_confidence = estimated.

validation_rules: If chain_length_from_governance_zone cannot be estimated even approximately, the claim does not meet the bar for the anchor registry. A genuinely unmeasurable chain length is a disqualification signal, not a null case.
If independent_genesis.independent_source_exists is false, genesis_confidence_weight must be reduced or minimal and genesis_note must be populated.
Anchors with genesis_confidence_weight = reduced are valid but must be noted in the analysis confidence_flags.

proposition_table._description: One record per proposition extracted from the source document.

proposition_id.format: P-NNN; sub-propositions use P-NNN-a, P-NNN-b, etc.

verbatim_source_text.notes: Exact quote from source document. No paraphrase permitted.

source_location.notes: Page, paragraph, line, or section reference

normalized_claim.notes: Analyst restatement in unambiguous language for graph encoding

is_compound.notes: True if proposition bundles more than one independently evaluable claim

compound_resolution.notes: Required if is_compound = true. See compound_resolution schema below.

is_relational.notes: True if this proposition's truth value depends on the relationship between two other propositions rather than on an independent anchor. A relational proposition asserts something about how two claims relate — e.g., that one implies, contradicts, or qualifies another.

parent_proposition_a.notes: proposition_id of the first parent proposition

parent_proposition_b.notes: proposition_id of the second parent proposition

relation_type.notes: The structural relationship this proposition asserts between its two parents

relational_note.notes: Analyst statement of what the relationship claim is and why it cannot be evaluated against an anchor directly

secondary_tags.notes: Maximum 3. If more than 3 are required, the proposition must be decomposed via compound_resolution instead. See allowed values below.

anchor_mappings.notes: Which anchors this proposition is evaluated against. At least one required. Relational propositions map to the anchors of their parent propositions.

geodesic_distance_from_anchor.notes: Null until graph computation pass is complete

stress_energy_contribution.notes: T_uv contribution; null until field computation pass is complete

confidence_flag_id.notes: Links to a record in confidence_flags if flagged

primary_classification_allowed_values.anchored_true: Directly supported by registered anchor, short geodesic chain.
primary_classification_allowed_values.inferentially_true: True but dependent on long inferential chain from anchor; elevated fragility.
primary_classification_allowed_values.bridge_narrative: Geometrically identified as boundary node with bidirectional T_uv load; primary classification is used when bridge structure is the defining characteristic of the proposition.
primary_classification_allowed_values.ambiguous: Cannot be classified without additional resolution; must have a corresponding confidence_flag record.
primary_classification_allowed_values.out_of_scope: Not addressable by registered anchors; noted but excluded from graph computation.

secondary_tags_allowed_values.contextually_misleading: Technically true but placed or framed to imply a false inference.
secondary_tags_allowed_values.selective_omission: True by itself but material context has been removed.
secondary_tags_allowed_values.false_precision: Quantitative claim stated with more precision than the evidence supports.
secondary_tags_allowed_values.bridge_adjacent: Not itself a bridge narrative but structurally dependent on one.
secondary_tags_allowed_values.bridge_narrative: Carries bridge structural characteristics alongside an independent primary classification; used when the proposition has a classifiable primary identity but also functions as a load-bearing boundary node.
secondary_tags_allowed_values.unattributed_agency: The proposition describes an action, decision, or outcome but does not name the agent responsible. The omission of the subject is the rhetorical move — passive construction or nominalization is used to remove accountability from the claim while preserving its apparent factual content.

decomposition_note.notes: Analyst statement of what was separated and why

primary_classification_basis.notes: Which sub-proposition drives the parent's primary classification, or 'composite' if no single sub-proposition is dominant

graph_structure._description: Poincare disk graph encoding the manifold structure of the document.

nodes.notes: One entry per proposition plus one per anchor

edges.notes: Signed edges encoding support and contradiction relationships

manifold_zone_assignments.notes: Nodes grouped by Poincare zone: governance, stable, contested, boundary

fragmentation_detected.notes: True if graph resolves into incompatible metric spaces

polarization_detected.notes: True if two positions found within one shared manifold

bridge_narrative_candidates.notes: Highest-stress boundary nodes identified prior to geometric summary

geometric_summary._description: Narrative description of the manifold structure. No verdict field. No conclusion field. No classification of rhetor intent.

manifold_description.notes: Shape of the belief space the document constructs

anchor_cluster_summary.notes: How anchored claims organize in the space

highest_stress_region.notes: Where T_uv is largest; described geometrically

least_stable_truth_candidate.notes: True claim with longest inferential chain; most vulnerable to bridge injection

most_stable_false_candidate.notes: False claim sitting closest to governance zone by geodesic measure

analyst_notes.notes: Optional. Non-binding. Explicitly outside the geometric summary. Any content here must not contain interpretive conclusions about intent, judgment, or verdict. Schema violation if it does.

reader_interpretation_note.notes: Verbatim-locked. Any deviation is a schema violation caught by the audit trail.
reader_interpretation_note.value: This summary describes geometric structure only. Classification of intent or moral judgment is left to the reader.

ambiguity_source.notes: Mutually exclusive per record. If both apply, two records are created, one per type.

ambiguity_description.notes: Analyst statement of the specific ambiguity

ambiguity_source_definitions.textual: The source text itself is unclear, incomplete, or admits multiple grammatical readings; classification requires disambiguating the text itself, not just the claim.
ambiguity_source_definitions.classificatory: The source text is clear; analysts disagree on which classification category applies.

falsifiability_record._description: Top-level field. One entry per extraction rule applied during analysis. This field constitutes the auditable record that the bidirectional falsifiability test was executed, and that a kill switch was defined before analysis began. The record must be populated before the analysis pass is committed.
falsifiability_record.enforcement: An analysis pass may not be committed if any applied rule has falsifiability_confirmed = false and no modifications_after_test entry explaining the discrepancy.

rule_id.format: RULE-NNN; matches the rule identifier in the methodology document

rule_summary.notes: One-sentence statement of what the rule does

uncomfortable_output_direction_a.notes: Documented example of a case where this rule would classify a true claim as false or ambiguous — the uncomfortable output on the side that risks over-flagging truth

uncomfortable_output_direction_b.notes: Documented example of a case where this rule would classify a false claim as true or ambiguous — the uncomfortable output on the side that risks under-flagging falsehood

kill_switch.notes: Required. A single sentence stating the specific observable data point that would force the analyst to declare this rule miscalibrated rather than the document anomalous. The kill switch must name a concrete, checkable condition — not a general category of failure. Example: 'If a known non-contentious historical fact such as the ratification date of a treaty triggers all four bridge signals, the manifold zone assignments are miscalibrated and this rule is falsified.'

falsifiability_confirmed.notes: True only if both uncomfortable outputs were documented, the kill switch was defined, and neither caused the rule to be discarded or silently modified

modifications_after_test.notes: Required if rule was modified after seeing the uncomfortable outputs or defining the kill switch; must state what changed and why

audit_trail._description: Top-level field. Immutable append-only log. Covers all post-commit writes to any field in any other section after the initial extraction pass is committed.
